source_surface: Arena paper, live leaderboard, policy page, historical dataset
claim_role: Human-preference leaderboard method and cutoff-bounded historical snapshot
allowed_caption: Use Arena only when the chart names the category, split, publish date, snapshot source, and uncertainty/rank-compression caveats.
blocked_claims: Live best model, durable superiority, all-task quality, safety, adoption, product value, price-quality, or broad intelligence.
required_caption_fields: benchmark; category/filter; publish date; snapshot id; model list; uncertainty metric; excluded rows; source ids
next_action: Keep S-0080 historical dataset as the preferred chart source; use S-0056/S-0057 as method/policy surfaces.

source_surface: Artificial Analysis methodology and index/leaderboard surfaces
source_type: methodology page and dynamic benchmark pages
claim_role: Independent aggregate benchmark and cost/latency/routing context
allowed_caption: Use Artificial Analysis as a methodology/snapshot surface only when version, evaluation suite, model release date, and access time are visible.
blocked_claims: Neutral intelligence crown, live model rank, price-quality frontier, provider recommendation, production performance, or full multimodal capability.
required_caption_fields: methodology version; evaluation list; weights; access date; time window; provider/model ids; cost/latency basis
next_action: Capture methodology page plus chosen benchmark page; do not cite current top rows without a dated snapshot.

source_surface: LLM performance leaderboard embed with speed, latency, and price definitions
allowed_caption: Show that speed, latency, and price rows are live measurement surfaces with definitions and time windows.
blocked_claims: Provider margin, actual cost, enterprise ROI, adoption, universal quality, or stable price history.
required_caption_fields: access date; measurement window; metric definitions; provider/model ids; cache/input/output blend; source url
next_action: Use only for methodology and snapshot candidates until row-level capture exists.

source_surface: SWE-bench paper, official site/leaderboard, and SWE-bench Verified announcement
claim_role: Repository-repair benchmark and verified-task caveat surface
allowed_caption: SWE-bench can support repository-repair evaluation framing only with variant, scaffold, tool access, date, and pass@k conditions named.
blocked_claims: General coding skill, autonomous engineering, productivity, security, job replacement, raw-model ability, or live coding crown.
required_caption_fields: variant; split; scaffold; tools; attempts/pass@k; run date; submission source; task count; evaluator conditions
next_action: Add official leaderboard/snapshot row before any numeric chart.

source_surface: LiveCodeBench paper and official leaderboard surface
claim_role: Contamination-aware code benchmark and freshness/cutoff caveat
allowed_caption: LiveCodeBench supports discussion of contamination-aware and time-windowed code evaluation only when date window and task type are shown.
blocked_claims: Best coding model, production code quality, security, workplace productivity, all-language skill, or stable live rank.
required_caption_fields: release window; task source; language/task type; tools; sampling; date; leaderboard snapshot id
next_action: Capture official leaderboard surface before using ranks; paper remains method source.

source_surface: Terminal-Bench 2.0 paper or official benchmark source
claim_role: Terminal-agent task harness and scaffold caveat
allowed_caption: Terminal-Bench can appear as a terminal-task benchmark only with task count, environment, tools, scaffold, run date, and pass criteria named.
blocked_claims: Autonomous operations reliability, production incident handling, general agent competence, best coding model, or enterprise productivity.
required_caption_fields: version; task count; environment; tools; scaffold; pass criteria; run date; evaluator; source ids
next_action: Use for C-0013 normalization and Claude 4 benchmark caveats.

allowed_caption: MMLU can support historical benchmark language only as a scoped multiple-choice task set across named subjects.
blocked_claims: General intelligence, reasoning, truth, product quality, current rank, or saturation without updated methodology evidence.
required_caption_fields: task count/subjects; answer format; model/date; prompting/scoring rule; source ids
next_action: Use as root surface for benchmark genealogy, not as a modern crown.

allowed_caption: GPQA can support hard expert-written Q&A framing when the variant and scoring protocol are named.
blocked_claims: Google-proof permanence, expert-equivalent reasoning, truthfulness, current rank, or all-science skill.
required_caption_fields: variant; question count; domain; answer format; model/date; prompting/scoring rule; source ids
next_action: Pair with model cards only after extracting exact variant.

allowed_caption: MATH can support math problem-solving benchmark history with dataset scope and scoring caveats.
blocked_claims: Proof of logic, broad reasoning, solved mathematics, current rank, or all-domain mathematical competence.
required_caption_fields: dataset split; difficulty scope; answer format; evaluator; model/date; source ids
next_action: Use as benchmark lineage and blocker surface.

allowed_caption: HumanEval can support early code-evaluation history for function synthesis from docstrings with test-based scoring.
blocked_claims: Production software quality, repo-level engineering, security, broad coding replacement, or current model rank.
required_caption_fields: task type; tests; pass@k; model/date; source ids
next_action: Keep as early code benchmark rung beside SWE-bench/LiveCodeBench.

allowed_caption: TruthfulQA can support the idea that models may mimic common falsehoods in a scoped benchmark.
blocked_claims: General truthfulness, safety, deployed behavior prevalence, universal model reliability, or current rank.
required_caption_fields: task set; scoring rule; model/date; source ids
next_action: Use in trust chapter only with blind-spot caveats.

source_surface: Figure-ready existing diagrams plus new source-surface slots
allowed_caption: A benchmark exhibit should teach permission: what the benchmark measures, what it does not measure, and what fields make a score citeable.
blocked_claims: Any one benchmark proving frontier rank, adoption, business value, safety, or real-world productivity.
required_caption_fields: benchmark; version; date; model; scaffold; tools; metric; source; blocker
next_action: Use this board to choose 10-15 final benchmark exhibits later.
